text.title: JPA / Hibernate Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Spring Data JPA + Hibernate methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every `@OneToMany`, `@ManyToMany`, and `@ManyToOne` association MUST set (or inherit) `FetchType.LAZY`. Eager loading MUST be opt-in per query via `JOIN FETCH` or `@EntityGraph`.
rule.rationale: EAGER fetch is global and unselective; combining two EAGER collections triggers `MultipleBagFetchException` or Cartesian explosions under load.

rule.statement: Entities MUST implement `equals` and `hashCode` over a stable business key (e.g. `naturalId`, externally-assigned UUID) — never over the generated DB id and never via Lombok `@Data`.
rule.rationale: DB id is null before insert and changes proxy semantics; Lombok `@Data` walks lazy collections and triggers infinite recursion plus DB loads.

rule.statement: Every diff to an entity (`@Column`, `@JoinColumn`, type change, rename) MUST land in the same PR as a paired Flyway migration. `spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto` MUST be `validate` in `prod` profile.
rule.rationale: Without paired migrations, startup `SchemaManagementException` aborts the deploy or — worse — Hibernate auto-DDL silently mutates the prod schema.

rule.statement: Bulk update / delete repositories MUST annotate `@Modifying(clearAutomatically = true, flushAutomatically = true)`. Hand-written JPQL bulk ops MUST flush+clear the persistence context.
rule.rationale: Bulk JPQL bypasses the first-level cache; without `clearAutomatically`, subsequent `findById` calls return pre-update entities and silently corrupt logic.

rule.statement: Repository tests MUST use `@DataJpaTest` (slice) + Testcontainers. `@SpringBootTest` MUST NOT be used for pure repository tests. OSIV (`spring.jpa.open-in-view`) MUST be `false` in all profiles.
rule.rationale: `@SpringBootTest` boots the full context; slice tests run 5-10× faster, isolate schema issues from app logic, and force the LazyInitialization discipline at test time.

rule.statement: Controllers MUST return DTO projections, not JPA entities. Entity-to-DTO mapping MUST happen inside the transactional service layer, before the session closes.
rule.rationale: Returning entities to controllers crashes with `LazyInitializationException` once OSIV is disabled; DTO projections fix the contract permanently.
